<<Quantum Computing and Cryptographic Security>>

SECTION 1  
The expected number of qubits $ Q $ needed to factor $ N $ with a success probability of at least 0.99 can be derived using the formula for Shor's algorithm. The time complexity is $ O((\log N)^3) $, which depends on the number of qubits and the error correction required. Given the gate fidelity $ F $ and coherence time $ T_c $, the number of qubits must be sufficient to handle the quantum error correction and maintain the required coherence. The expression for $ Q $ is typically given by $ Q = \lceil \log_2(N) \rceil \times \log_2(1/F) \times \log_2(T_c) $, which accounts for the number of qubits needed to achieve the desired accuracy and coherence.
